UK Launches First Deep Geothermal Power Plant in Cornwall

🤖 IA: It's not clickbait ✅
👥 Usuarios: It's not clickbait ✅

#geothermal #renewableenergy #lithium 

View full AI summary:

external.description: The UK is set to inaugurate its first deep geothermal power plant in Cornwall, providing renewable electricity to around 10,000 homes. Developed by Geothermal Engineering Ltd (GEL) over nearly two decades, the project involved drilling the UK's deepest onshore well. Water heated by granite rocks underground will drive turbines to generate electricity, offering a continuous, 24/7 energy source unlike intermittent wind or solar power. The plant will also supply the UK's first domestic lithium, a critical mineral for batteries and green technologies. The £50 million project was funded through private investors and the European Development Fund. Octopus Energy will distribute the electricity via the national grid. While shallow geothermal technology, such as ground source heat pumps, is already used across the UK, deep geothermal remains rare due to high costs and technical challenges. Experts highlight that deep geothermal could provide stable green power and support growing energy demands from industries like data centers. GEL plans to expand to additional sites, although some face environmental scrutiny. The government has signaled support, appointing the UK's first geothermal minister. The plant represents both a renewable energy milestone and a domestic source for key minerals in the UK's green transition.
external.title: UK Launches First Deep Geothermal Power Plant in Cornwall

Swiss Government Announces Emergency Payout for Victims of Crans-Montana Bar Fire

🤖 IA: It's not clickbait ✅
👥 Usuarios: It's not clickbait ✅

#switzerland #fire #compensation 

View full AI summary:

external.description: The Swiss government has announced an emergency payout of 50,000 francs (approximately $65,000 or £48,000) for the victims of the deadly New Year's Eve fire at the Le Constellation ski bar in Crans-Montana. This solidarity contribution will be given to each of the 115 injured individuals and the families of the 41 people who lost their lives. Investigations indicate that the fire began when sparklers attached to champagne bottles ignited soundproof foam in the bar's basement, which was crowded at the time. Among the victims, eight were children under 16, and 58 people remain hospitalized with severe burns. Swiss President Guy Parmelin and Finance Minister Beat Jans stated that this payment is in addition to a previous 10,000-franc emergency aid offered by the canton of Valais and aims to provide rapid, bureaucratic-free support. The federal government is also offering up to 20 million francs for further compensation talks between victims, insurers, and officials. Local authorities admitted that the bar had not undergone safety inspections for five years. The owners, Jacques and Jessica Moretti, face charges of manslaughter, bodily harm, and arson by negligence, while legal proceedings also target the head of public safety and a former fire safety inspector in the municipality. Authorities emphasize their commitment to truth, justice, and understanding the cause of the tragedy.
external.title: Swiss Government Announces Emergency Payout for Victims of Crans-Montana Bar Fire
